content:
# Matthew Chapter 9 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/MAT_009_page_001.jpg, MAT_009_page_002.jpg

1. And having embarked into a boat, he crossed over and came into the own city.

2. And behold, they were bringing to him a paralytic, having been laid upon a bed. And having seen the faith of them, the Yeshua said to the paralytic, "Take courage, child; the sins of you are forgiven."

3. And behold, some of the scribes said within themselves, "This [one] blasphemes."

4. And having known the thoughts of them, the Yeshua said, "Why do you think evil things in the hearts of you?

5. For which is easier, to say, 'The sins of you are forgiven,' or to say, 'Rise and walk'?

6. But in order that you may know that the Son of the Man has authority upon the earth to forgive sins" — then he says to the paralytic — "Having risen, take up the bed of you and go to the house of you."

7. And having risen, he went to the house of him.

8. And having seen, the crowds feared and glorified the God, the [one] having given such authority to men.

9. And passing on from there, the Yeshua saw a man sitting at the tax-booth, Maththaion being called, and says to him, "Follow me." And having risen, he followed him.

10. And it came to pass, him reclining in the house, and behold, many tax-collectors and sinners having come were reclining with the Yeshua and the disciples of him.

11. And having seen, the Pharisaioi were saying to the disciples of him, "Why with the tax-collectors and sinners does the teacher of you eat?"

12. And having heard, he said, "The [ones] being strong have no need of a physician, but those having badly.

13. But having gone, learn what is: 'Mercy I desire and not sacrifice.' For I came not to call righteous [ones] but sinners."

14. Then the disciples of Ioannes come to him, saying, "Why do we and the Pharisaioi fast much, but the disciples of you do not fast?"

15. And the Yeshua said to them, "Are the sons of the bridal-chamber able to mourn as long as the bridegroom is with them? But days will come when the bridegroom is taken away from them, and then they will fast.

16. And no one puts a patch of unshrunk cloth upon an old garment; for the fullness of it pulls from the garment, and a worse tear happens.

17. Nor do they put new wine into old wineskins; otherwise the wineskins burst, and the wine pours out, and the wineskins are ruined. But they put new wine into fresh wineskins, and both are preserved."

18. These things of him speaking to them, behold, one ruler having come was doing-obeisance to him, saying that "The daughter of me just now died; but having come, lay the hand of you upon her, and she will live."

19. And having risen, the Yeshua followed him, and the disciples of him.

20. And behold, a woman bleeding twelve years, having come from behind, touched the fringe of the garment of him.

21. For she was saying within herself, "If only I touch the garment of him, I will be saved."

22. And the Yeshua, having turned and having seen her, said, "Take courage, daughter; the faith of you has saved you." And the woman was saved from that hour.

23. And having come, the Yeshua, into the house of the ruler, and having seen the flute-players and the crowd making-tumult,

24. he was saying, "Depart, for the girl did not die but sleeps." And they were laughing at him.

25. But when the crowd was put out, having entered, he grasped the hand of her, and the girl was raised.

26. And went out this report into that whole land.

27. And passing on from there, the Yeshua, two blind [men] followed him, crying out and saying, "Have mercy on us, Son of David!"

28. And having come into the house, the blind [men] came to him, and the Yeshua says to them, "Do you believe that I am able to do this?" They say to him, "Yes, Lord."

29. Then he touched the eyes of them, saying, "According to the faith of you let it be done to you."

30. And the eyes of them were opened. And the Yeshua sternly-warned them, saying, "See [that] no one knows."

31. But they, having gone out, spread-abroad [news of] him in that whole land.

32. And of them going out, behold, they brought to him a mute [man] being demonized.

33. And the demon having been cast out, the mute spoke. And the crowds marveled, saying, "Never was it appeared thus in the Israel."

34. But the Pharisaioi were saying, "By the ruler of the demons he casts out the demons."

35. And the Yeshua was going about all the cities and the villages, teaching in the synagogues of them and proclaiming the good-news of the kingdom and healing every disease and every sickness.

36. And having seen the crowds, he was moved-with-compassion concerning them, for they were harassed and thrown-down, as sheep not having a shepherd.

37. Then he says to the disciples of him, "The harvest indeed [is] great, but the workers [are] few.

38. Pray therefore [to] the lord of the harvest, that he may thrust out workers into the harvest of him."

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **ἐσπλαγχνίσθη (v.36):** "Was moved-with-compassion" — from σπλάγχνα (entrails, bowels). This is visceral compassion felt in the gut, not mere pity. The word describes a physical reaction of deep empathy.

2. **ἐσκυλμένοι καὶ ἐρριμμένοι (v.36):** "Harassed and thrown-down" — the imagery is of sheep that have been savaged by predators and left lying helpless. Some manuscripts read "scattered" (ἐκλελυμένοι) instead of "thrown-down." The Sinaiticus reading is more vivid.

3. **σωθήσομαι (v.21):** The woman says "I will be saved" — σῴζω means both "save" and "heal." The double meaning (physical healing = spiritual salvation) is characteristic of the Gospel narratives.

4. **v.13 — Hosea 6:6 citation:** "Mercy I desire and not sacrifice" — this is a key verse for Matthew's theology, cited again in 12:7. It defines God's priority as mercy over religious ritual.

5. **v.34 — Pharisees' accusation:** This accusation (casting out demons by the ruler of demons) is developed fully in chapter 12. Here it appears as a first mention — a narrative seed that will grow into the Beelzebul controversy.

— EMET, AI Translator
